message:
test(escape-parser): expand CSI and streaming parser test coverage

Add 1190 lines of new tests across the escape sequence parser,
dramatically improving coverage of CSI (Control Sequence Introducer)
handling and the incremental streaming parser.

csi.rs (+1085 lines):
Comprehensive test expansion covering terminal attribute sequences:
  - Blink attribute tests: verify CSI 5m (slow blink) and CSI 6m
    (rapid blink) set correct CellAttributes variants, and CSI 25m
    resets blink to none
  - Intensity attribute tests: CSI 1m (bold), CSI 2m (half-bright),
    CSI 22m (normal intensity) with state transitions between modes
  - Underline attribute tests: CSI 4m (single), CSI 21m (double),
    CSI 4:3m (curly), CSI 4:4m (dotted), CSI 4:5m (dashed),
    CSI 24m (underline off) — verifying the colon-separated sub-param
    syntax for extended underline styles
  - SGR (Select Graphic Rendition) compound tests: multiple attributes
    in a single sequence (CSI 1;4;31m for bold+underline+red),
    selective reset behavior, and attribute stacking
  - Cursor movement tests: CUU/CUD/CUF/CUB (up/down/forward/back),
    CHA (cursor horizontal absolute), VPA (vertical position absolute),
    CUP (cursor position), with default-parameter and explicit-parameter
    variants
  - Edge cases: empty parameter lists, maximum parameter values, mixed
    valid and ignored sub-parameters

parser/mod.rs (+105 lines):
Streaming parser tests validating incremental byte-at-a-time feeding:
  - Incomplete-then-complete OSC (Operating System Command) sequences
    split across multiple parse() calls to verify the parser correctly
    accumulates partial sequences and emits actions only on completion
  - Boundary conditions for escape sequence framing across chunk
    boundaries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
